segments.text: There was a man named Nicodemus, a Pharisee and a member of the Jewish ruling council.
He came at night to where Jesus was and said, {
Rabbi, we know you are a teacher who has come from God, for nobody could do the miraculous signs you're doing unless God was with him.
segments.speaker: Nicodemus

segments.text: I tell you the truth,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied,
Unless you are reborn, [^17] you can't experience God's kingdom.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: How can you be reborn when you're old?
segments.speaker: Nicodemus

segments.text: Nicodemus asked.
You can't go back into your mother's womb and be born a second time!
segments.speaker: Nicodemus

segments.text: I tell you the truth, you can't enter God's kingdom unless you are born of water and the Spirit,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus told him.
What's born of the body is body, and what's born of the Spirit is Spirit.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Don't be surprised at my telling you, ‘You must be reborn.’[^18]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The wind blows wherever it wants, and just as you hear the sound it makes, but don't know where it's coming from or where it's going, that's how it is for everyone who is born of the Spirit.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: How is this possible?
segments.speaker: Nicodemus

segments.text: Nicodemus asked.
You're a famous teacher in Israel, [^19] and yet you don't understand such things?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
I tell you the truth, we speak of what we know and give evidence regarding what we have seen, but you refuse to accept our testimony.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you don't trust what I say when I tell you about earthly things, how would you ever trust what I say if I were to tell you about heavenly things?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: No one has gone up to heaven, but the Son of man came down from heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Just as Moses lifted up the serpent in the desert, [^20] so the Son of man must be lifted up,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: so that everyone who trusts in him will have eternal life.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For God loved the world, and this is how:[^21] he gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who trusts in him shouldn't die, but have eternal life.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: God didn't send the Son into the world to condemn the world, but to save the world through him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Those who trust in him are not condemned, while those who don't trust in him are condemned[^22] already because they didn't trust in the one and only Son of God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: This is how the decision[^23] is made: the light came to the world, but people loved the darkness rather than the light, for their actions were evil.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: All those who do evil hate the light and don't come into the light, because they don't want their actions to be exposed.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But those who do good[^24] come into the light, so that what God accomplishes in them can be revealed.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: After this Jesus and his disciples went into Judea and spent some time with the people, baptizing them.
John was also baptizing at Aenon near Salim, because there was plenty of water there and people kept coming to be baptized.
(This was before John was imprisoned.)
An argument developed between John's disciples and a Jew over ceremonial purification.
They went to John and told him, {
Rabbi, the man you were with on the other side of the Jordan River, the one you testified in support of—see, now he's baptizing, and everyone is going to him!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: No one receives anything unless they're given it from heaven,
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: John replied.
You yourselves can testify that I declared, ‘I'm not the Messiah. I've been sent to prepare his way.’
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: The bridegroom is the one who has the bride! The best man waits, listening for the bridegroom, and is so happy when he hears the bridegroom's voice—in the same way my happiness is now complete.
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: He must become more important, and I must become less important.
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: He who comes from above is greater[^25] than all; he who comes from the earth belongs to the earth and talks about earthly things. He who comes from heaven is greater than all.
He gives evidence about what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts what he has to say.
Yet anyone who accepts what he says confirms[^26] that God is truthful.
For the one God sent speaks God's words, because God doesn't restrict the Spirit.
The Father loves the Son and has placed everything in his hands.
Anyone who trusts in the Son has eternal life, but anyone who refuses to trust the Son will not experience eternal life but remains under God's condemnation.
